channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon? — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon?

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-04-17 19:08 UTC

OP

You DID secure your moon bag…right, anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62125227
>>62124756
they will get turned away, even if the crowd lined up at the current levels! there will only be 21 million coins and many people have more than one already. the crowds will be turned away since there is not enough of them left. i read that there are already something like 200k bitcoin holders and only a million coins left to be mined. even if each of those new coins went to a new holder (they won't) the maximum number of people that can have a bitcoin at the same time is 1.2 million.

62125283
I placed an order last weekend and initiated an ACH transfer to buy more tomorrow.
t. lucky retard

62125298
>>62125283
>lucky retard
well, half of that statement is true.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125310
>>62124756
It will dump when the ceasefire fails (like it always does). Hopefully you sold the fake pump… You did sell the pump, right, anon..?

62125355
>>62124756
change 67k to $500 and 1M to +50,000

62125410
>>62124756
i did buyed

62125446
>>62125310
Yes

62125582
>>62124756
It’s hilarious how dumb people are to think BTC is even worth the 20x considering btc’s product. BTC’s original use was decentralized anonymous currency and it’s become neither decentralized nor anonymous. It’s defied the very thing it set out to do while there are better coins working to become more centralized and other coins that are more anonymous. BTC only caught up once it became more centralized. You want massive banks and governments backing your crypto money laundering scheme and if you know one better than BTC, I would invest in that before BTC because it’s bound to have better margins. Bitcoin has only become a hedge for better, more lucrative coins, ironically enough.

62125583
>>62125227
>21 million coins
>21,000,000 x 100,000,000 satoshi
>2.1 quadrillion
Oh no

62125597
>>62125298
thank you

62125644
>>62124756
If that many normal people are buying bitcoin at £1M it means currency has hyper inflated so much that thats cheap and poorfags can afford it and not only that every poorfag has £1M for bitcorn something has gone wrong, Also what if the new Chinese order decide to just turn it off by decrypting the entire network and dumping the private keys for lols

62125672
>>62125310
It won't dump until July.

62125766
>>62125310
Selling is for fags. Just buy weekly and hold until it hits a million. If it fails (it clearly won’t) then oh well. You lost a gamble.

62125827
>>62125582

>it’s not decentralized
There are 70,000+ nodes worldwide enforcing bitcoins rules. Unless you think 1 guy secretly owns them all?

>its not anonymous
Good luck getting widespread adoption and ETFs for your monero

>it succeeded because it became more centralized
You have this backwards.

I wish you luck with your altcoins but your post is incoherent. It’s bad because it became more centralized while other coins are good for becoming more centralized?

62126111
>>62125583
you think more than 1.2 million people can own a bitcoin at the same time?

the supply is capped, there is simply not enough of them for the crowd. maybe they will be able to rent one for a time but by and large the people that are going to have a bitcoin already do.

62126137
>people like OP turned bullish at the pico top of the bear flag

yikes

62126166
>>62126137
Zoom out nigger. We aren’t selling til $5m

62126217
>>62124756
I picked xrp because $1 -> $1 million is a million x whereas $100k -> $1 million is only 10x

62126224
>had my buy orders at $63k
>dip barely clears $65k
Fuck I'm pretty sure I didn't miss the boat but this price action is demoralizing.

62126314
Why people would rush to buy Bitcoin at 1 mi when they didn't at 100k?

62126338
>>62126314
kek

if we even reach 200K next bullrun consider yourself very lucky

62126414
MY STRATEGIC BITCOIN RESERVE NOW HOLDS 0.22997 BTC!!!!!!

item.title: Crude Oil — 21 replies — 81.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crude Oil

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-04-17 19:00 UTC

OP

>there was some anon on this Business & Finance board, posting multiple angry posts and crashing out, while arguing in all seriousness, with 100% sincerity, that oil would go to $220
>two hundred united statesian American dollarinos
>there were multiple ESL foreigners posting in agreement, in fact the majority of the thread was anons crashing out

My fucking sides are in orbit. Panicans absolutely blown the FUCK out once again! NEVER gets old!

Early replies (first 3)

62125480
they imploded futures with direct treasury manipulation
the trade got crowded and volatile
the result was inevitable

some people trade without thinking that others also have access to the info they just read online
they don't think "my neighbor could be thinking the same thing"
so they crowd the same sale

62125514
>>62125480
Reeks of cope

/biz/ is just full of retarded brown foreigners, at the end of the day. Very important to keep this in mind when taking any advice from this board. The smartest posters made money and left.

62125548
>>62125514
oil is being phased out
not even by green energy
look at how much GW the United States is building in NatGas power plants to fuel data centers

this was a short term trade
anyone thinking about second, third, forth order effects are completely out of the loop
even the Iraq war made oil spike through a year and resumed dumping after a year
and that had boots on the ground

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125572
>>62125548
>"Oil is going to $200"
>No it's not, you're a retard
>oil dumps to $80

>"Rather than admitting I was wrong and that I am a retard, here's a bunch of meaningless drivel about why I was actually correct (even though I was objectively wrong)

I don't care about your cope, dude. Oil was never going to $200 and a bunch of retarded brown foreigners were posting multiple threads about how oil was going to go ballistic and how obvious it was that oil was going to $200, etc.

You were wrong, and that was obvious from the beginning. Deal with it

62125593
the issue as usual is Trump being a nigger lying about shit to pump the markets

62125601
>>62125572
>Rather than admitting I was wrong
but I didn't trade oil
I was agreeing with you, little bro, go read my posts again lol
chill out

62125799
>>62125548
>oil is being phased out
that's the fate of all non-renewables. because they run out

62125819
You retards larping as humans has me seriously considering buying oil before market close.
It is a Friday after all.
In the middle of the economic part of WW3.
What did Mr orange pedophile say again?
Yeah, do the opposite.

62125837
>>62125593
What will be the next fud to turbodump the markets? Like, that October 10th tariff announcemen tlevel?

62126271
>>62125819
Go for it faggot. $200 in two weeks. Just two more weeks!

>>62125593
>oil didn't go to $_____ b-because... Trump's lying!

cope

>>62125480
>>62125548
The way I see it, you're concurring with me, but you're also making excuses for retards, as if they were ever even on the right track. Their thought process was very simple and very wrong. They didn't get cheated out of a directionally correct trade because it was overcrowded, or because it was a short term trade vs. long term, etc. That's cope. They made (or likely, just talked about making) an emotional trade based off of anger, resentment, and wish-casting. Now that the market has proven them dead wrong, they're coping & seething, and still making excuses for being wrong. See: several posts in this very thread.

62126301
Isn't spot price (which is the real price) $150?

62126316
>>62125389
I voted for this

62126330
Oil baggies rn

62126337
>>62126301
>Isn't spot price (which is the real price) $150?

the absolute state of this board

62126340
wtf is spot price

62126345
actually i don't care i can't afford to trade futures xd

62126355
>>62126301
Where are you getting that number from?
https://www.eia.gov/todayinenergy/prices.php

62126368
more the day goes on the more convinced i am nothing has changed, oil goes back up and this was just more bullshit shakeout to fuck over speculators (me)

62126381
>>62126301
OIL BAGGIE SPOTTED

LEARN THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN SPOT AND FUTURES YOU RETARD

62126386
I'd still sau it's a 50% chance that the srait gets blocked again over the weekend. I wish i had anything to sell

item.title: HE CAN'T KEEP GETTING AWAY WITH IT — 6 replies — 20.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
HE CAN'T KEEP GETTING AWAY WITH IT

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-17 18:58 UTC

OP

HE CAN'T KEEP GETTING AWAY WITH IT

Early replies (first 3)

62125961
>>62125946
BOBO!!!! BOBO LOOK OUT BEHIND YOU!!!

62126067
>>62125961
its too late

62126119
>>62125946
oh man what a cycle. frontran the high. frontran the low.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62126148
>>62125946
>I CAN CHANGE!

62126230
>>62125946
Mumu stays classy

62126374

its over for bobo kek

item.title: Hedera (HBAR) — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hedera (HBAR)

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-17 19:10 UTC

OP

What went wrong?

Early replies (first 3)

62124523
>>62124464
> posts reddit slop
> doesn't elaborate

It's so cringe it's almost based. Anyway, I still don't know what HBAR does. With other failed shitcoins I sort of have a clue. ALGO is dApps & DiFi (both dead), ICP is Caffeine and muh internet 3.0 (useless Claude wrapper, dead, hosted on Amazon), Sometimes ICP scam-pumps. XRP is FedWire replacement (everyone uses USDT instead). HYPE is exchange on chain iirc (no one cares, DiFi basically). I don't really know what LINK or HBAR do. I am not even joking, something about sergey, oracles, space marines and then something about Lemon and solved trifecta. Like I literally have no clue what HBAR does or who actually uses it.

62125359
>>62124464
well we didnt see bull market that went into 2026. That's the end of it.

62126379
>>62124523
I use HBAR to buy E and pr0n

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62126419
>>62124523
Same here. I heard HBAR is going to boost from Clarity if it ever passes, but I don't know what the fuck it is still.

item.title: >I make 100k a year — 14 replies — 14.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>I make 100k a year

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-17 18:16 UTC

OP

No sweetie, after your good goy deductions you make 65k. Even VA disability fairys make more then you

Early replies (first 3)

62124195
>>62124134
Post your W2

62124334
brvtal trvke

62124638
>>62124134
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62124654
I made 200 after taxes :)

62124886
>>62124134
I make around 120k after taxes and have VA disability

62124902
>he doesnt reduce his taxable income in any meaningful way
yikes

62125084
>>62124134
Took me some time to figure out why wagies strictly talk about brutto income, at least in Czech Republic that's the case. The truth is simple - because they're poor and they are coping by including money that's not theirs.
>"I make 50k CZK brutto monthly!"
No wagie, you receive 37k in your account, the rest is taxes. You have never seen 50k in your account.
Netto is the only relevant amount anyone should be mentioning. I can't imagine "flexing" with brutto. Are these people bragging about how much they get fucked by taxman? Couldn't be me.
>"b-b-but you have to calculate netto!"
I know exactly how much I really get paid, wagie. Maybe if you too knew how to count, you wouldn't be so fucking poor.

62126161
i make like 250 after taxes while living in bongland

62126174
i make nothing
>>62124654
>>62126161
can you even start a career in current year & end up making that much?
it seems like everything is gatekept by faggots who want dey "job" security at the expense of everyone else

62126178
it also seems like a lot of jobs are basically slut welfare at this point

62126189
>>62126174
i graduated less than 3 years ago

62126191
i guess there are probably a lot of exceptions, including many trades, but idk

62126196
>>62126189
ah nice

i may just be coping honestly, i dropped out of HS
not sure what to do & i don't trust anything/anyone

62126197
move to a zero income tax state ASAP bros. we still have roads and shit lol.

item.title: I am just over halfway through a week long fast — 8 replies — 9.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
I am just over halfway through a week long fast

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-17 18:26 UTC

OP

If I don't make it, LINK will never pump. Passed the hardest day at least, day 3. However my girlfriend is having her friends over this evening for take away, which will be my next challenge.

Early replies (first 3)

62124722
supremely based. why have you decided to fast?

62124768
>>62124641
im ending my 3 day fast tomorrow evening

62124777
your gf is aware of this and having her friends over?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62124919
>>62124722
Thanks bro. I've fasted in the past, lots of OMAD, some shorter 2-3 day fasts, my longest and latest being 5.5 days, so wanted to hit a week.

I always feel great after them and I had let shit go a little bit. Straight into slight caloric deficit after and I'm hitting the gym 5x times a week now, so just kick-starting with this and taking back control.

>>62124768
Fuarrkk let's go brah, let's pump link through fasting

>>62124777
Wasted trips, it was organized way before I personally decided to fast.

62125032
>>62124919
i've done 3 day fasts, a few times. will 7 days be much harder?

62125123
>>62125032
I mean, I still have just over 3 days to go, and yes there's been hard times, especially end of day 3 for me.
Today feels way easier though, just gotta stay on top of electrolytes and distract yourself from thinking of food.
Hunger pants have mostly gone at this point thankfully.

I remember at the end of the 5.5 day one thinking I could have kept going, but my goal had been 5 days.
It's not easy, but it's a trip

62125129
>>62125123
*Pangs lol

Should lost about 2kg of body fat by the end.
And with water weight about 6-7kg (that comes back as you refeed)

62126248
I dropped down to 92kg from 115kg since August last year. Was pretty easy to start with just cutting out chocolates/snacks/crisps and only having fast food occasionally but now it's slowed down.
Feeling better but aiming at 80kg and around 15% bodyfat, so started hitting the gym and tracking my calories for a 500kcal deficit, wish me luck bros.
Tired of being a fat lethargic slob

item.title: XRP – Inverted Reality($17, $27, $35 & $42): Flip the chart… and everything changes. — 7 replies — 8.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP – Inverted Reality($17, $27, $35 & $42): Flip the chart… and everything changes.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-17 18:24 UTC

OP

XRP – Inverted Reality($17, $27, $35 & $42):

Flip the chart… and everything changes.

I’ve done the inversion, now it’s on you to define the targets.

Are you seeing structure… or still trapped in noise?

Structure > Noise

ONLY FEW UNDERSTAND

Early replies (first 3)

62123899
KABOOOOOOOM

62125564
>>62123851
Wow, if you flip the chart on btc it looks like a line gradually going down
If you flip it on shitcoins it still looks like insider trading

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125650
>>62123851

62125768
>>62123851
I see you bagholders have started RETARDMAXXING.

62126146
>>62123865
Why do you post this in every XRP thread? Who is it?

62126236
>>62126146
some schizo retard who is going to be rich if he doesn't cuck out

AMC bro

AMC bro

How do I buy a call????????

62126378

item.title: BTC Wallet recovery — 7 replies — 6.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Wallet recovery

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-17 18:10 UTC

OP

Hey, on my external HDD i found my university study data which one of files was BitcoinWallet, i recognized in 2011-2012 at cryptography classes we had BitCoin practices, each student used BitCoin Core and got some from professor and we were exchanging them each other. Im not sure if anything is there (at the end we could send everything back to professor) but i hoping even for 0.1 BTC.
BitcoinWallet file content was in Base64 so i decoded it into output.bin
output.bin content started with Salted__8 so GPT said its encrypted in OpenSSL, i was able to decode it be guessing my old passwords.
Next part was downloading BitCoin Core and this part when im stuck.. its been already few days, one of my RAM memory is malfunctioning and being far from completed (620gb) it brokes to the point where not even reindexing helps to keep progress and i had to start everything from beginning 3 times... also my slow HDD not helping.
What is the easiest way to recover public key (wallet address?) from this wallet just to check if its worth hussle.

Early replies (first 3)

62122704
If there's anything left you should return it to your professor since it's property of the university

62122732
>>62122704
Sorry dude but wallets were our personal, not university property.

62122791
>>62122692
Your post is too ESL and I can’t figure out what your exact problem is. Go ask chat gpt or have it fix your grammar

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62122895
>>62122692
You could try this
https://github.com/bitcoinj/bitcoinj

Someone else posted they successfully recovered the same wallet format as yours using this java app

https://www.reddit.com/r/Bitcoin/comments/1i8oq5f/wallet_file_with_base64_encoding/

62122971
>>62122692
Kek so your professor tricked you all into mining BTC for him paid for with govt tax dollars.

Im kinda not even mad, fuck it the systems a shitshow anyway, get yours.

62124665
why would you even do a full bitcoin core sync if it's from 2012. Stop the synch after 2012
And you don't even need to sync at all to look at the wallet addresses. Balances can be checked on a block explorer.
Post more info about the wallet file. What does the output look like? don't post anything that looks like gibberish or like private material

62126167
>>62122971

Back in the day it was worth cents and very easy to mine.
Not at all obvious it would explode.

item.title: XRP weekly macd is about to have its first bullish cross since last July. — 4 replies — 3.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP weekly macd is about to have its first bullish cross since last July.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-17 18:07 UTC

OP

You are not bullish enough.

62123629
>>62123547
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62123635

Stop mocking Satoshi

62123890
>>62123547
KABOOOOOOM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62126157
>>62123547

item.title: Trump won — 2 replies — 2.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Trump won

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-04-17 18:28 UTC

OP

and yet his shitcoin is barely pumping. This should have been a watershed moment for this turd.
It should be pumping 5x of what SOL is pumping for today. Memes are all about the narrative and the narrative is about Trump being victorious.

Early replies (first 3)

62125762
>>62125300
They told me this coin had no value but joke's on them, I can use it as a loss to offset my capital gains

62126251
based

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: I have been fired lol — 29 replies — 56.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have been fired lol

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-04-17 18:45 UTC

OP

After 10 years working in a big corpo. I went to my office today and the security guard was there. He didn't let me touch anything and escorted me to HHRR but I already knew what was going to happen.
They didn't give me any reasons. My boss didnt even look at my eyes. They don't want to go to court so they paid me a HUGE severance pay + TWO years of full unemployment benefits (EU country)
And now i'm just here sitting in my living room still processing that after a decade I won't need to wake up early and fight with my bosses and customers anymore. It feels weird.
I probably will take a sabbatical in SEA or something.
Holy shit. It really feels weird.
What would you do /biz/? I honestly dont care about a big gap on my resume and money is not an issue now

Early replies (first 3)

62125619
>>62125608
lol

62125630
What are you good at? Capitalize on your hobbies with seed money from the severance package.

62125698
>>62125630
>Capitalize on your hobbies
>Hiking, filming and traveling.
Oh great i'm going to be a youtuber now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62125743
>>62125608
Sounds like a country with high taxes. What are the rules for taxes if you're outside of the country? Become a trader, open a business, etc. Get into DeFi if your country is trying to screw you on taxes.

62125760
>>62125698
I like that, in death, all this kike cared about was getting a swipe in at another jewtube kike. What a fuckin loser lmao

62125804
>>62125608
Where's the party, anon?

62125813
>>62125608
>HUGE severance pay + TWO years of full unemployment benefits
i'm jealous

62125875
>>62125743
>Sounds like a country with high taxes
If I legally sell my BTC bag these bastards will take 23%, so yes. But no trading for me. I'm terrible at it.
>>62125804
Pattaya? Im still deciding. But today im going to eat a good steak and get drunk at home.
>>62125813
>i'm jealous
Don't be. It took a DECADE of corporate hell to get this, and piss off a regional CEO enough to not even want to go to court with their army of lawyers. They just wanted me OUT as soon as possible.
Damn, what the fuck did i do lmao.

62125887
>>62125875
1>5>2>4>3

62125891
it’s crazy how bloodless it all is. and now with so many remote or hybrid workers they’ll just schedule a Teams meeting and fire you while you’re at home. you ship back your computer and that’s it. I work for a big US company that has done a lot of rounds of layoffs over the past 6 years. luckily my department was only affected by one of them. my boss’s boss DMd me asking who the two weakest performers were. I gave him two names and they were gone the next day. I think each got 6 months of pay. we just had another round of layoffs last week and I was lucky to survive again. by I’m constantly waiting for it to happen because they’re already in the process of outsourcing most of our work to South America. eventually I won’t be needed.

62125902
Don't get too comfy, you should start finding out what to do now instead of waiting until you need to start looking for a job because you wont find one. I bet if you tried to find a job starting now you would finally get hired 9 months from now.

62125917
>>62125875
5>2>1>3>4

62125920
>>62125608
I sold a business and had some time before getting a job. I was depressed when unemployed.

62125938
>>62125875
Can you take a BTC loan and pay zero taxes on it? Then you can invest the money in a business or something and pay back the loan. There are services that will auto-payback the loan in case BTC goes down so that way you don't get liquidated.

62125952
>>62125875
2>5>3>1>4

62125958
>>62125902
>Don't get too comfy, you should start finding out what to do
You know, after a decade of corporate hell (and before that, years of office wagecucking) is really that bad that I want to take a sabbatical year? I'm 38 so I know that it's a bad idea, but my parents are starting to be really old and maybe this is the last opportunity to do it before I have to take care of them...
also i'm single since Christmas
>>62125938
Are you trying to sell me some shady loan website or something? Anon I don't need risky investments anymore, I have enough money for years and my btc bag won't leave my trezor ever

62126015
>>62125958
Just take some time off, man. No one's going to bat an eye at a one-year gap on your resume, sabbaticals are perfectly acceptable in Europe and you're still young enough to have fun.

62126016
>>62125958
No DESU I'm just literally in your exact situation however I want number to continue to go up instead of number go down. Safe APY is good, BTC is good, trading whatever you feel safe trading is good, and starting businesses is good.

62126026
>>62125608
Plenty of time for investment research. 7 months after I first began looking around I saw a crypto recommendation for a new chain. Then October 2023 happened and everything dipped. It came back from that dip and by 2025 it had become a 10000x. Sure not every year has that kind of opportunity but the point is that it's good to be looking around even if you're not staring at charts of "in the trenches" 24/7.
>SEA
Wherever you go from here but especially there, be sure to look up the history of natural disasters and be prepared for them so you don't end up having to pay even more money due to being unprepared. Pic-related, you never know what's possible. Also, get into growing your own food and having a back-up home. This isn't about an apocalypse, but being prepared for temporary but harsh scenarios. I have a pal who plays games for hours upon hours per week yet still gets more out of his greenhouse than he can eat by himself, so don't shy away due to the initial time investment. Many people throughout history who were well off enough to not have to work, were suddenly hit by economic/environmental disasters and really felt it due to being unprepared. But maybe according to your research and where you live, no such things will happen in your lifetime. The important thing is to look into it.

62126029
>>62125608
Happened to me. I got another job within 3 weeks and got to choose my favorite. 15 years experience C/C++ is pretty employable turns out. Thinking about quitting anyway to go fly airplanes as a second career instead.

62126079
>>62125875


2>5>1>3>4

62126106
>>62125608
That must feel alright. My work time seesaws between 4 months of nonstop all day work and about 4 months off and it feels good to not work for 4 months.

62126110
>>62125608
>losing your job
good
>losing your house
good
>wife leaves you
good
>getting cancer
good

all of these things are good. it will make you stronger. you will come back stronger. I unironically envy you, wish I would lose something important to feed my drive.

62126116
>>62126029
>halo
>fly planes
Reminds me of Joe

62126134
>>62126015
> Europe
Yeah I was about to say, if OP was in burgerland, even a 1 week sabbatical will absolutely destroy your resume and make you permanently blacklisted by ANY hiring company in this abysmal job market.

62126253
>>62126110
Gee thanks siddharta

62126254
>>62126134
Just set your LinkedIn Employment Status to CTO of Stealth Startup. HR still hasn't figured out that means you were actually bug chasing in SEA for a year.

62126303
>>62125608
>2 years pay for getting canned
You better make the most of it you lucky bastard.
>>62125875
I don’t care what you say, I’m still jealous. I know I wouldn’t receive such a nice payout if I was fired. If you don’t at least spend a nice year doing everything you put off, you’ll regret it. Fuck.
-t. wageslave amerifag

62126319
>>62125608
>I probably will take a sabbatical in SEA or something.
GAAYYYYYYYYYYYY
do something more interesting with your life/time holy fuck

nice job though anon
>Holy shit. It really feels weird.
>What would you do /biz/? I honestly dont care about a big gap on my resume and money is not an issue now
depends on other factors

item.title: HoW iS eVerYonE dOinG this? — 104 replies — 25.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
HoW iS eVerYonE dOinG this?

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-04-17 15:11 UTC

OP

I love watching normie carhag/carfag compilations where Normies complain about how everything is too expensive.

I live on ~24k a year in a HCOL city making $26/hr
>$820 rent utilities included
>free transportation (fare fags aren’t checking train during my commute hours)
>$300/mo groceries (steak and potatoes mostly)
>$60/mo unlimited data
>$15/mo rent insurance
>$30 dental insurance *shrugs*

I regularly spend $8 on coffee, $15 on cigs, $30 dollars on pizza, and I STILL usually dump minimum $1500 a month into my brokerage account. Next month my credit limit will go from 12k>24k when I open another chase CC. I have 6 months expenses in a savings account and the equivalent in dry powder in my brokerage.

Being completely unstressed/concerned about money and whipping out my girthy 12k CL sapphire preferred card on cutie coffee sluts 4 times a week to order my $9 dollar 24oz drum of double chocolate mocha and then ripping a Newport 100 on a comfy bench while i enjoy my coffee is all the pleasure I need in this world

Early replies (first 3)

62107190
It's funny because I'm supposed to be an Europoor but my life standars is better than 99% of Americans lmfao.

I've never heard a single European complaining about debt. But you meet one of those Amerimutts that come to our continent for tourism or to teach English, and 5 minutes into the conversation they will start telling how how much in debt they are. BAFFLING.

62107202
>>62107169
>HCOL city
>$820 rent utilities included
and then your dad stopped molesting you and the out of body disassociation cope ended.

62107317
>>62107169
>>62107190
I'm American. Envious, ungrateful, whiny, bitchy Americans are an embarrassment. It's really not hard to live here at all. The problem is almost every retard that lives here thinks you should be able to afford a 2,300 sqft house on 1 acre of land, while having a new Toyota Camry in the driveway and enough in savings to go on 2 - 3 week-long vacations a year after putting 15% into an investment account, all on a McDonald's wage, or a basic-bitch corporate cubicle job, while only working 30 hours a week (yes, even 40 hours is too much work for Americans now).

Most people live WAY above their means. It's just a mixture of low-IQ combined with a set of personality traits that make them susceptible to short-term thinking and hedonism. Then they beg for free-gibs from the government while producing less material goods and services. It doesn't take a rocket scientist to figure out where this leads to.

I feel zero pity or sympathy for any of these people. Fuck them and their retarded choices.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62119779
OP here, thanks to whoever revived my thread
>>62110818
Good luck family man
>>62111774
Correct bucko
>>62114448
>>62114452
Coping chimp alert
>>62114990
Nope, right here.

Rent is 820 and the rest is on the ole chase sapphire. So I can see exactly what I’m spending. Don’t go too crazy trying to hone in on any particular number for my coffee budget or cigs etc. the bottom line is I spend about 2k a month on everything incl. rent.

It’s a 120sqft “””efficiency””” studio that’s why the rent is so cheap in this HCOL city.

62119791
>>62119779
>Rent is 820
>It’s a 120sqft “””efficiency””” studio
WTF? I mortgage a 1,000sqft house for $1,100/month.

62119962
>>62119791
You probably live in Ohio or one of those “fucking nowhere places”

I live in a city where farting in wine glasses and whiffing it would seem normal

62119967
>>62119962
>I live in a city where farting in wine glasses and whiffing it would seem normal
Skill issue

62120037
>>62119962
>I live in a city where farting in wine glasses and whiffing it would seem normal
New Delhi?

62120104
>>62119967
>>62120037
Alright ALRIGHT fellas listen. Its seattle. Yes, that’s right. I’m rubbing shoulders with tech dorks from Microsoft AND google.

I bet some of them don’t even have a sapphire preferred

62120111
>>62107169
>12k CL sapphire preferred card
Jeets think a 12k credit limit is alot. Nigga, I OWE that on just one of my cards.

62120298
>>62120111
NO WAY WHAT
SHE REALLY IS A HE

62120353
Saving and investing while living with the rents works. Im 34 and I’m damn nearly a millionaire and all I’ve done is be a zogbot and work gov or gov contractor jobs, but I have more liquid assets than friends who went into tech that inflated their COL

62120364
>>62111774
Well then clearly he doesn't live off 24k a year since his expenses alone are more than that. After taxes he's bringing in probably around 48k, and his margin of error for spending is super low. I highly doubt he's being real as a smoking habit would easily eat into any excess funds he has.

>>62119779
>120 sq ft
Ah I see, your memeing that you own nothing and are happy. Makes sense

62120406
>>62112085
Are you old and retarded? You can get a smartphone for like 300 bucks that will last 5 years. And jobs expect you to have them. Your bank uses apps, your job will use a app for timekeeping, you will get emails with paperwork to fill out before end of the day with a digital signature. It is a source of music vs cds dvds and a player, it gets rid of the maps on your car you used to have to buy, you don't need to buy a watch, etc.... literally for 300 bucks. For a device that your will be paying the bulk of the monthly for just a basic phone. Unlimited for me is 35$/month. 3 gigs was like 25$ which is the plan i would use if i had a flip phone. Ffs you can get a free smartphone if you are on ebt. If people took your advice, they would probably end up with shitty assets and the inability to be hired anywhere but starbucks or under the table garbage.

62121038
>>62120406
> Your bank uses apps
Banks do have apps, but you can still do all your banking on a computer.
> your job will use a app for timekeeping, you will get emails with paperwork to fill out before end of the day with a digital signature.
Not my problem. I don't work a wagie job.
> It is a source of music vs cds dvds and a player
Why pay subscription fees for music when I can load up an old iPod with all the music I want and can listen to it all without an internet connection
> it gets rid of the maps on your car you used to have to buy
GPS devices for cars exist. They're often more accurate and lower cost.
> you don't need to buy a watch
Watches are based. Only Zoomers and thirdies think otherwise.
> If people took your advice, they would probably end up with shitty assets and the inability to be hired anywhere but starbucks or under the table garbage.
Ramblings of an illiterate, brain-dead Zoomer who can't imagine life without eight hours of daily screen time on his phone.

62121080
>>62107387
Fucking LARP
I literally live right off MLK way in Seattle and one bed studios start at like $1200 a month. I live in a 2 bedroom that costs me $1900/month before utilities
Either you and OP are bullshitting or you have no conception of what a HCOL city really is

62121133
>>62121080
he's LARPing. you can't even get a studio in a nigger shithole like camden, nj for under $1k a month.

62123272
>>62107190
Yeah but what kind of car do you drive? How is your garbage disposal? Is your refrigerator taller than you are? Do you have a laundry room with washer and dryer?

Americans are retarded but earn more and buy more to debt hard.

62123382
>>62120353
I lived with my father until he died two years ago and now live by myself in the house. it definitely helped me save a lot. I'm 42 and between a taxable investment account, 401k, and cash I am around 1.5. the problem is you have to have no social life for this strategy to work long term. I would trade it all away in exchange for having had a normal social life and a wife, kids, etc.

62123416
>>62120353
Woa man you’re doing great.
>>62121080
Cope I can’t believe a fellow seattleanon would do me like that. You’re making me feel smug as fuck. There are no lies. It’s not a standard studio you dingbat. No stove, no bathtub, yet I’m grateful. Airfrying Chuck steaks and microwaving greens in my silicon steamer thing.

I’m living large pal.
>>62121133
*smuggies multiply*

Don’t seethe guys. I legit found my living situation on Reddit.

Oh and anons? I don’t pay for the 2 line to get to and from work :) I’m actually riding it now. We at Justin’s park
>>62123382
You could still get a SEAbunny wife

62123420
>>62107169
> I live on ~24k a year in a HCOL city making $26/hr
That’s not HCOL lol
HCOL would require $150k plus

62123428
>>62123420
Cope. Only Normie retards need 150k. I’m a friendless loser

62123431
>>62123428
You just said you live in hcol. Being able to survive on 24k means youre not in a hcol area.

62123432
>>62123428
I don’t think you know what hcol or vhcol is. I live in a vhcol area. I don’t need to cope.

62123434
>>62123428
This isn’t about normie or friends. We are clearly focusing on hcol or not.

62123439
>>62123431
>>62123432
>>62123434
Ok ferrets, yes or no: Seattle is a HCOL city. It’s regularly in the top 10

I’m gonna need all of you to go fuck yourselves

62123446
>>62123439
WHERE in Seattle. The ghetto areas?
Each city had vhcol areas then hcol then mcol and etc.
24k is lower class income. And that anon is saying he lives in hcol area. He probably lives in a lcol area in an expensive city. It is like how even LA or nyc had cheap areas.
Like come on man. You’re arguing with me when that anon already said he makes 24k.

62123451
>>62123439
Seattle? Sure, depends on neighborhood. Tf is wrong with you? Youre poor and so desperate to say you live in “muh hcol” city.

62123458
>>62123439
This board is like 10% business discussion contributed by 10% of its posters.
I can’t believe youre even arguing with me AFTER the guy said he makes 24k. Lmao. You make yourself look so stupid and this userbase.

62123461
>>62107169
>newport
are you even white? wtf are you doing on this website. gtfo.

62123464
Your city might have “expensive city” reputation but you clearly live in a cheap area with ghetto people. Assuming you didn’t make a troll thread.

62123466
>>62107190
America is a debt based economy.
You are a cuck based economy.

62125196
>>62123451
Oh be fucking quiet trying to limp along with your shitty point by partitioning up my city into neighborhoods. Holy shit. It’s not even that big. The blacks live in Kent and Tacoma so don’t even start.
>>62123464
No I don’t dumbass I can walk 15 minutes and I’m downtown

item.title: Why aren’t you holding BTC? — 109 replies — 25.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren’t you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 109 • Updated: 2026-04-17 14:59 UTC

OP

If you’re still watching or waiting to buy BTC, why not do it now?
>I guess you’re wasting time, or maybe you just prefer altcoins. What’s your reason?

Early replies (first 3)

62087977
>>62087937
I only watch it to laugh at Saylor and you bitsteinbaggies. Not buying digital nothing.

62087993
>>62087937
Waiting for sub60K

62087997
>>62087937
I don't have enough money for it to make me rich. Alts have a good chance of vastly outperforming BTC during the next big pump. I've decided to take the potential for a 10x over the guarantee of a 2x.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62106279
>>62106216
Its quite obvious desu

62106298
btc is going to 20k if not lower

>>62097398
>'good alts' will vastly outperform
have you been asleep for 5 years?

there was like 3 alts that outperformed btc out of literally millions. your odds are terrible for alts, much better chance to just throw it all on red and call it a day

62106312
>>62106298

62106435

62106463

62107082

You're not smart enough.

62108172

62109768
>>62097398
Sure, chase “good alts”… just don’t cry in a few years when they roundtrip to zero and BTC is sitting way higher.

You’ll wish you just held the hardest asset instead of playing ETF cosplay.

62110223
Guarantee 30% return on semiconductors and useless AI tech or gamble that Bitcoin crawls back to 100k any time soon despite losing 40% of it's value in the last few months. Hmmmm

62110242
I expected that until December Bitcoin will sunk to below 40k and Solana will be around $20. I will buy solana and Hold it until 2029 when the next bullrun will start.

2027-2028 will be crab market, everyone will say that crypto is dead just like 2023.

I will buy SOL because it will probably 20x while BTC will only 5x.

62110338
>>62102233
A 17 year old in the prime of their life.

62110351
>>62110338
Fact, can't wait to back kick some zombies

62110380
>>62087937
Why aren't you holding nano?

It literally has perfected digital money.

>I guess you’re wasting time, or maybe you just prefer inferior coins. What’s your reason?

62112997
>>62110380

62114412

62114414

62114891
>>62110223

62114897

62117929

62117990

62119044
>>62117990

62123333
>>62093341
check clearpool for long, kusama for short term

62123643
>>62088069
I love these seething retards hahaha

62123772
>>62123333
just started, don't sell now

62124465
>>62097429

62124708
>>62123333

62124800
I don't see the point now
I'm not investing for longevity and it's reached a plateau

Would have been worth it years ago but I had no money and couldn't convince my retard family to invest

Id rather gamble on altcoins with 10x potential

62124932
>>62087937

62125025
>>62124932
Ah gold
>useless in socialist managed decline as you will not retain asset value when selling, if they don't just confiscate it
>useless in societal collapse as soon as the new civilisation is formed because they will just take it and you won't have more guns or dudes to stop this

item.title: The strait is still closed btw. Sending the US navy to block it up even harder only makes things worse This pump is comp… — 42 replies — 24.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The strait is still closed btw. Sending the US navy to block it up even harder only makes things worse This pump is comp…

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-04-17 17:33 UTC

OP

The strait is still closed btw. Sending the US navy to block it up even harder only makes things worse

This pump is completely artificial and designed to trap as many plebs as humanely possible

Early replies (first 3)

62120115
open the fuckin strait

62120217
>>62120101
10.000 kbbls/d oil is missing, fertilizer even worse. People going to start walking somewhere if they are hungry and no relive in sight.

62120280
>>62120101
Why not just nuke Iran and move on? Nobody likes them anyways.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121548
>>62120217
What are kabibbles per d?

62121556
>>62121548
bbls = barrels
k = 1000x
d = day

62121575
>>62121556
Copy that.

62122036
>>62121556
bbls = blue barrels

Standard Oil was the biggest game in town for a long time, and their oil barrels were painted blue, and rapidly became the standard barrel size in the industry. Oil barrels of that size became known as "blue barrels" whether they were actual blue-painted Standard Oil barrels or not.

62122395
>>62121326

62122517
Brer Trump punched the Tar Baby. Comical shit.

62122624
>>62120291
>i know basic news everyone knows
>i know less than investment banks, hedge funds, governments

Banks and NPCs: famous for being correct about everything and never being blinded by greed.

62122745
>>62120101
Bear gf

62122816
>>62122745
Bear wife

62122824
>>62120101
well my option expires in less than 24 hours so it won't matter

62122860
i wish a hot girl would hug me

62122901
bobina is a fucking WHORE

62122909
>>62120291
>>62122624
you write like women

62122948
>>62120101
I'm always astounded by how beloved Bobina is by this board but that Mumette, Crabitha, and Swanica are all rarely used comparatively.

62122959
Yeah but yknow, if I sold right now, I'd win.

62123008
>>62121505
>>62121508
>>62121520
what in tarnation
i still don't understand what happened with the previous short squeeze a few years ago, or what a short squeeze is

62123162
>>62122948
its literally one guy posting it, so he can subtly shill his coin.

62123172
>>62120101
i would pay $2m to be bobo in this pic

62123206
>>62120101
>humanely possible
>humanely
>human

62123257
>>62123008
>someone sees something is poised to do badly, think "this things fucked 100%", bets on it doing badly by going to somebody with shares, telling them to sell today and give them the money from the sale, and in X days they will come back and give them the exact amount of shares they had sold for them (you want them to be cheaper to buy back so you make money)
>The thing in question potentially doesn't get fucked, and infact goes higher than the price it was sold at for them
>Puts the short seller in a shitty position to where they either have to put more money up to close their short sale via either closing and buying the shares at the higher price, or having to put up more collateral while paying interest to keep the short open in the hopes that it swings back in their favor (what that previous short squeeze you eluded to continues to do and continues to obfuscate)
>Is very bad when the short sale in question is done in excess of both the short sellers funds (leverage) and the amount of shares that are available (float) / exist (outstanding shares)
>worse still if/when the entity facilitating this transaction calls you and says "give me my fucking money" (margin call), because they themselves are broke because multiple other entities are doing the exact same thing to them and theyre technically liable for moving "the bag" around (and they can be margin called by their lines of credit)
>further compounded by whoever gets sold the shares then turns around and lets somebody else short them off their hands and so on and so forth ad infimum
>sometimes there aren't even any shares to begin with and they sell off phantom shares that you think you own and are real but are just complete bullshit because the person selling them to you couldn't find it / were unable to borrow it
>This happens all day everyday across every stock and every market while the dtcc (the central counterparty that has to pay everybody out) does absolutely fuck all
Hope this helps!

62123339
>>62123008
When you go short, you MUST buy at some point to close the position.
When you short a stonk, you're borrowing the stonk from someone else and immediately selling at current price. You have to re-buy that stonk at some point and return it to the lender (to close the position).
When you open a short position in futures, you are obligating yourself to sell something for <x> dollars a pop. You need to have the thing in the first place in order to sell it (duh). So if you don't have it to sell, you'd have to buy it in order to fulfill your obligation to sell.
You might not think of it that way, but that is what's happening, mechanically.
Ok.
A short squeeze is when the market looks at something that's massively shorted. The market sees this and says, "If we push price high enough, those shorts will all be stopped out and forced to BUY at a loss to close their position."

When BYND rallied last year, part of the motive was that there were so many shorts on BYND that "if we can hit their stop losses, it will trigger a colossal series of market buy orders."
When silver rallied earlier this year, part of the motive was that "Comex has shorted more silver than it can afford to sell IF price were high enough."
When GME happened so many years ago, part of the motive was "GME is massively shorted, and we want to hit their stops."

So America has been shorting oil to keep price down. Tamping it, as /pmg/ faggots say. This comes with a caveat: eventually America will have to execute all these short contracts. And if oil price is up bigly, oil will be short squeezed.
Hope this make some sense at all.

62123352
>>62123257
>>62123339
ty, ty

62123353
>>62123008
>>62123339
In short (HA HA), a short squeeze is a stop hunt directed at short positions.

62123357
Bobo, where my hug?

62123361
>>62120280
Why not just nuke Israel and move on? Nobody likes them anyways.

62123369
>>62120115
We don't need the strait

62123378
>>62123206
Bearbinaly?
>>62122901
She is not. She is a loyal wife and mother. She is known for teasing tho

62124116
>>62120101
I dont care.

62124225
>>62120280
This is one of the Iranian missile bases that exercise fire control over the strait of Hormuz. It's under 200-400 meters of hard rock. Try nuking it and see how it goes.

62126020
>>62124225
No need to nuke it, the US blew it up a month ago.
